The General Service Officer had not yet been designated.

There is a significant delay in the submission of perfected contracts and supporting documents.

observations.reason: Two (2) statutory DRRM positions were not filled.

Salaries of SB Members were still not supported with documents proving attendance in sessions. Moreover, the Audit Team did not receive most of the job orders’ or contracts of service’ accomplishment reports.
There is still delay in the submission of accounting reports and transaction documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Treasurer to ensure the prompt processing and submission of paid vouchers and other necessary financial documents to the Municipal Accountant’s Office. Likewise, the Municipal Accountant should ensure the timely preparation and submission of the monthly BRS, along with the corresponding bank statements and related documents, within the prescribed period.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Designate a General Service Officer to take custody of and be responsible for all municipal properties, through a formal office or executive order;
Require the Inventory Committee to prepare and submit RPCPPE no later than January 31 of each year; and
Direct the Municipal Accountant’s Office, General Services Office, and the Inventory Committee to reassess their implementation of COA Circular No. 2020-006 and develop an action plan ensuring full compliance, including scheduling adjustments, proactive coordination with COA auditors, and contingency measures for personnel absences.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to ensure that the receipts of fund transfers, grants and donations from other levels of government, private sectors, or international institutions are recognized as revenue for proper matching of the revenue and expense accounts.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Require the RHU to promptly inform the Supply Officer of all deliveries of drugs, medicines, and medical supplies from the DOH and PHO;
Direct the Supply Officer, upon notification by the RHU, to:
Accept and inspect the delivered items.
Complete the PTRs prepared by the donor, particularly in the “Receiving Facility’s Action” section (e.g., Quantity Received, Remarks, and/or reason for rejection), and sign the “Received/Acknowledged By” portion of the report.
Coordinate with the donor regarding the valuation of items for which no cost is indicated.
Forward copies of the duly accomplished PTRs to the Municipal Accountant for recording and monitoring purposes.
Record the items in the stock cards; and
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to record the donated inventories in the books and supply ledger cards based on the submitted PTRs.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant properly classify tax liabilities under the Due to BIR account in accordance with the Revised Chart of Accounts and IPSAS 1. The Municipality should also maintain accurate and complete records of tax remittances to avoid difficulties during audit.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive, as Co-Chairperson of the LSB, should:
Ensure the timely approval of the SEF budget by strictly adhering to the deadline set under DepEd-DBM-DILG JC No. 1, s. 2017, which requires approval not later than October 31 of the current year, by prioritizing early coordination among LSB members to prevent delays in budget preparation and submission;
Require the LSB Secretary to ensure the timely submission of the SEF Budget to the Municipal Budget Officer, Municipal Accountant, and Municipal Treasurer within the prescribed period, in accordance with the JC timelines; and
Direct the Municipal Accountant and LSB Secretary to comply with the submission requirements for SEF utilization reports by ensuring that quarterly and annual reports are properly submitted to DepEd, the Sangguniang Bayan, DBM, and DILG within the prescribed deadlines.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Direct the Municipal Development Council to conduct thorough site assessments and engage stakeholders early in the planning process to ensure proper project feasibility and reduce the risk of project relocation;
Initiate discussions with the Sangguniang Bayan and other key stakeholders to expedite the resolution of pending budget realignments and ensure efficient fund reallocation to priority projects; and
Strengthen the project monitoring committee through the regular conduct of monitoring activities and timely coordination with implementing offices to immediately address delays and other issues affecting project implementation.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Direct the Municipal Accountant and HRMD-in-charge to attach accomplishment reports to salary vouchers for job order workers to substantiate the necessity of LDRRMF utilization.
Instruct the Municipal Budget Officer to ensure the LDRRMF is used strictly for activities aligned with disaster risk reduction and management, and charge disbursements for KALAHI Office job order workers whose tasks are misaligned with the appropriate fund source.

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to ensure that the position of the three staff responsible for the administration and training, research and planning and operations and warning be established in the regular Plantilla item, in accordance with NDRRMC, DILG, DBM, and CSC JMC No. 2014-1 dated April 4, 2014.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant and concerned offices to conduct an immediate review and reconciliation of the LBP Account No. 0802-1107-90. This includes verifying the balance per book, determining the necessity of maintaining the account and coordinating with the Source Agency for the proper disposition of funds. If the account is no longer necessary, the unutilized balance should be returned to the Source Agency as required by funding agreements or transferred to the General Fund for appropriate reallocation, subject to applicable laws and regulations. Moving forward, measures should be implemented to prevent the recurrence of dormant accounts by ensuring the timely utilization of funds and regular monitoring of trust accounts.

We recommended to the management to institutionalize the use of ATM payroll system for the payment of salaries, wages, and other benefits instead of cash advances for enhanced internal control, economical and to mitigate the risk of fund loss and ease the burden on the disbursing officer.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the BAC Chairman and Secretariat to ensure the timely submission of perfected contracts with complete supporting documents by clearly assigning responsibility for document preparation, tracking deadlines, and ensuring completeness before submission, while also addressing staffing constraints through internal reassignment and avoidance of conflicting duties that contributed to the delays.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive establish a GAD M&E system through the issuance of an Executive Order to ensure that GAD activities are properly monitored and that gender-related initiatives effectively benefit the community.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Direct the Municipal Accountant to:
▪ Conduct verification and analysis of the balances of the Due to NGAs accounts;
▪ Coordinate with the Municipal Engineer in determining the financial and physical status of programs, projects, and activities sourced from the National Government and other government agencies; and
▪ Reconcile the balances of Due to NGAs account with the balances of concerned source agencies and effect the necessary adjustments;
Communicate and make representation with source agencies for the return of unutilized balances related to completed projects; and
Direct the focal persons of the program/projects/activities particularly the Municipal Planning Coordinator and the Municipal Engineer to coordinate with the source agencies on the necessary action to be taken with the funds received for which intended programs, projects, and activities remain unimplemented or, if implemented, were not completed or abandoned for whatever reasons.

3.5	We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Treasurer to establish a proper cut-off in remittance collections and require Collecting Officers to accomplish the RCDs at the end of each business day according to the provisions of COA Cir. No. 92-382 and Sec. 29 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System for Local Government Units.

4.5	We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Treasurer to regularly monitor the sufficiency of fidelity bonds of the Collecting Officers and to require an increase of fidelity bonds should it be necessary.

5.18	We recommended that the Local Chief Executive revisit and observe COA-DBM-DILG-GCG-DND Joint Circular No. 2015-01. Further, instruct the Municipal Budget, Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to adhere the following:
For the release and use of the CF
Prepare the Physical and Financial Plan;
Secure Resolution duly approved by two-thirds (2/3) of the total membership of the Local Peace and Order Council; and
Secure Certification from the concerned PNP Chief in the locality relative to the peace and order situation highlighting in concrete detail the circumstances that require the urgency in allocating CF.
For the grant and liquidation of cash advances
Cash advance vouchers shall be sealed in an envelope to the COA ATL along with the covering transmittal letter and the required supporting documents.
The LR shall be submitted together with the supporting documents and the covering transmittal letter to the ICFAU.
CF shall be used only for the allowed CE.
For the submission of the report, submit Quarterly Accomplishment Report on the use of CF to the Secretary of the DILG.

6.10	We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the BAC Chairman through the BAC Secretariat to ensure that perfected contracts and the supporting documents thereof are submitted on time, in accordance with COA Circular No. 2009-001, for a timely review and evaluation of the contract and rectification be made possible should there be defects/deficiencies noted.

7.7	We recommended that the Municipal Mayor as the Chairman of the LDRRMC include the unexpended balance of the LDRRMF of the previous years in the LDRRMFIP in order to utilize the same to fund LDRRM PPAs and direct:
The LDRRMO
Prepare and submit supplemental LDRRMFIP/LDRRMP for all programs, projects and activities (PPAs) to be charged from unexpended LDRRMF to LDRRMC for approval; and
Submit the approved LDRRMFIP/LDRRMP to the Municipal Development Council for deliberation and inclusion in the AIP;
The Municipal Accountant
Ensure that the release of unexpended LDRRMF is with adequate documentation and authorization in accordance with the provisions of R.A. No. 10121, COA Circular No. 2012-02 dated September 12, 2012 and NDRRMC-DBM-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1 dated March 25, 2013.

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive the following:
Require the Vice Mayor and members of the SB to cease granting financial assistance, adhere strictly to the aforecited regulations, and act as legislators of the Municipality. Endorse or recommend the requests for financial aid from their constituents to the Office of the Municipal Mayor for the Municipal Social Welfare and Development Office to evaluate the request and for appropriate action;

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the Local Chief Executive to: 

Direct the Municipal Budget Officer to:

Refrain from using the “Discretionary Fund” account since it is not in the Revised Chart of Accounts per IPSAS and COA Circular No. 2015-009. Appropriation for the discretionary purposes of the Local Chief Executive are to be provided using the EME account, which in no case exceeds two percent (2%) of the actual receipts derived from basic RPT in the next preceding CY and that no other amount be appropriated for the same purpose;

Appropriate the budget for the expenses not falling under the description of EME to a more appropriate account(s); and

Direct the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjustments to reclassify the various expenses charged against and recorded in the EME account that do not fall within its description to fairly present the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Implemented
observations.reason:
The Municipality has discontinued the practice of maintaining separate appropriations for Discretionary Expenses and EME.

The Municipality has properly appropriated its budget for EME.

The Municipal Accountant refrained the usage of EME account.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendation
We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to:
Direct the Municipal Accountant to record Real Property Tax Receivable/Special Education Tax Receivable at the beginning of the year which will be credited upon actual receipt of payment pursuant to Section 20 of the Manual for Manual on the New Government Accounting System for Local Government Units;
Require the Municipal Treasurer to furnish the Municipal Accountant of the duly certified list showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year which will serve as basis for the latter’s Journal Entry Voucher (JEV); and
Require the Municipal Accountant and Treasure to ensure reconciliation of their data thereafter.

Recommendation:
We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to:
Direct the Municipal Accountant’s Office, General Services Office and Municipal Engineer’s Office to properly reconcile their records to ascertain the reliability and validity of the balances reflected in the financial statements, and submit reports which are thoroughly reviewed to ensure reporting consistency, data reliability and comparability. Furthermore, these three departments should have proper communication, e.g., by having effective data access/sharing, to avoid any further unreconciled items as well as deviations in asset classifications among reports;
Direct the Municipal Accountant’s Office and Municipal General Services Office to carefully take into consideration the proper treatment of the significant amount of unserviceable, lost and demolished properties noted. Persons accountable for those lost properties must be correctly established to act out necessary measures as well as to provide the appropriate accounting entry and avoiding direct derecognition of assets from the books of accounts.
Direct the Municipal General Services Officer to prepare and submit request for technical evaluation of disposal for unserviceable properties and request for relief of accountability in a timely manner for prompt response from the concerned agency;
Direct the Municipal Accountant’s Office and General Services to revisit the guidelines and procedures for one-time cleansing for the of PPE account balances specifically in the conduct of physical count of PPE, recognition of PPE items found at the station, and disposition for non-existing or missing PPE items, per COA Circular 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020, and to finally address the non-establishment of the accuracy of the PPE balances presented in the financial statements; and
Lastly, direct the GSO to maintain accurate and updated property records, prepare ARE evidencing the receipt of the person in custody of the property, and make regular renewal of the same for all the properties of the City.

Recommendation:
We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to:
Create an Inventory Committee to conduct a complete physical inventory count over the LGU’s Local Road Network. A physical count of Local Road Network should be done at least once a year and prepare Report on the Physical Count of Road Network System and submit the same to the Office of the Auditor;
Direct the Municipal Engineer to provide the Municipal Accountant, General Services Officer with the complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects;
Direct the General Services Officer to maintain the Local Road Inventory and Road Map, and to keep a complete Local Road Network Property Card for all roads and its components; and
Require the Municipal Accountant the following reporting procedures:
Keep and maintain subsidiary records for roads and road components for every road network; and
Prepare a lapsing schedule for the computation of the depreciation for each component at the end of the year.

Recommendation
We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to:
Submit valid justification for submitting only the signed payroll to support the P1,776,000.00 and P906,000.00 disbursements for the TUPAD Program implemented by the LGU;
Submit the required supporting documents per DOLE D.O. 219 series of 2020 and COA such as valid IDs, TUPAD IDs, contract of service, certification from the LGU that the beneficiaries have performed the work under the TUPAD Program, certification that individuals noted to have almost the same data are not from the same family, Memorandum of Agreement, SB Resolution authorizing the LCE to enter into MOA to avail DOLE Programs, certification from the Accountant of no unliquidated cash advances and/or grants from DOLE, and all other necessary documentations; and
Strictly ensure that the transactions of the LGU are supported with complete documentation that would ascertain its propriety and validity.

Recommendation:
We recommended to the Local Chief Executive the following:
Require the Municipal Accountant’s Office to coordinate with the General Services Office and reconcile their records to ascertain the reliability and validity of the balances of inventory accounts reflected in the financial statements.
Direct the General Services Office:
To remind all departments of the LGU to prepare and submit regularly the necessary SSMIs with the original copy of the RIS to GSO for proper consolidation and timely submission to the Accounting Department.
To maintain accurate and updated property records for each type of supplies by having Stock Cards per stock number. Difference between the balance per stock cards and per inventory count must be investigated and cleared immediately.
Direct the Municipal Accountant’s Office:
To maintain updated perpetual inventory records comprising of Supplies Ledger Cards (SLC) for each commodity/stock and properly observe the provisions for recording of inventories and the corresponding recognition as an expense through preparation of JEV using appropriate expenditure accounts.
Create a committee to validate and certify the existence and/or issuance of the inventory items that are in the possession of the Municipal Government but are not recorded in the books of accounts, and submit a corresponding report thereon to support a request for an authority from COA for dropping of inventory items from the books of accounts.
Remind all departments of their accountability, responsibility and liability for handling government property and inventories, and direct them to maintain and submit records/reports of their respective monthly inventory balances reflecting actual receipts and issuances for a more efficient reconciliation, inventory taking and other supply management procedures, including but not limited to RPCI.

Recommendation:
We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to:
Limit the use of RER for transactions or expenses amounting to more than P300.00 but not exceeding P1,000.00 purchased from or rendered by establishment not issuing receipt or invoice pursuant to COA Circular 2021-001; and
Prioritize purchases of the Municipality from duly registered establishments which can issue official receipts to provide support or evidence of receipt that is duly acceptable in audit.

Recommendation:
We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to:
Ensure constant maintenance check on the system’s software and hardware requirements; and
Designate an IT personnel who will be trained to do the maintenance and troubleshooting to avoid disruption in the operations of the Municipal Treasurer’s Office.

Recommendation:
We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to:
Stop the practice of shouldering taxes for procurements made through reimbursements;
Minimize transactions pertaining to reimbursements through proper procurement planning; and
Strictly avoid incurring irregular and unnecessary expenses as provided under COA Circular 2012-003 dated October 29, 2012.

Recommendation:
We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to:
Ensure thru the Property/ Supply Officer/LGU Inspector to accomplish the pre-repair inspection report after the conduct of pre repair inspection of properties subjected for repairs and furnish a copy of this report to COA Auditor within five (5) days from the date of evaluation/inspection. Likewise, when the subject properties were already repaired, conduct immediately post-repair inspection and submit a report thereon; and
Direct the Municipal Accountant to require the attachment of the Waste Materials Report and repair history in all payments for repairs and maintenance of vehicles and similar nature as part of the supporting documents.

Recommendation:
We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to direct the Municipal Accountant to submit the lacking disbursement vouchers complete with relevant supporting documents and to keenly observe the deadline set for the submission of trial balances and other financial reports as required under Section 122 and 127 of PD 1445, Section 7.2.1 of COA Circular 2009-006, and pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 95-006.

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive the following:

Direct the MSWDO to provide a written justification on the inclusion of the seventy-six (76) replacement beneficiaries in the distribution of SAP.

Require the MSWDO to submit to the audit team the MOA signed by the DSWD Regional Director and the Municipal Mayor

Mandate the MSWDO to always address the concerns relating to qualification, acceptance or replacement of target beneficiaries by the processes of eligibility assessment as stated in DSWD Memorandum Circular No. 04, Series of 2020 on Special Guidelines on the Provision of Social Amelioration Measures by the DSWD.
observations.managementAction: Requested a copy of the MOA from DSWD Region X.
observations.reason: Lack of personnel in facilitating the necessary compliance to the recommendations provided.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to:

Direct and remind the BAC Secretariat on the preparations and posting of reports for Bayanihan Grant required under Section 9 of GPPB Circular No. 01-2020 and

Direct the Local Finance Committee and MDRRMO to submit the Annex C of DBM LBC Circular No. 125 to COA and to post the same to appropriate website for wider public viewing.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to require the Municipal Treasurer to:

Maintain a Check Register, by fund, where all checks issued shall be recorded chronologically and where the claimants shall be required to acknowledge receipt thereof;

All checks issued, whether released or unreleased, including cancelled checks shall be recorded chronologically in the appropriate Cashbook; and

At the end of the year, a Schedule of Unreleased Checks shall be prepared for submission to the Accounting Office.

observations.reason: An elected official was still granted CA other than for traveling expenses.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to direct the Inventory Committee to complete the actual physical inventory in accordance with COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020, comply with the general and procedural guidelines as set forth in the said Circular and submit the Annual Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE).

Executive to Direct the Municipal Accountant to strictly follow the rules and regulations on the granting and liquidation of cash advances as prescribed under COA No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997 specifically:

Recommended the LDRRM Council to prepare annually the LDRRMF Investment Plan in its prescribed format and include the unexpended balance of the LDRRMF of previous years in order to utilize the same to fund LDRRM projects/ programs/ activities.

Recommended the Management the following:
     1. Require the Supply Officer to file a request for relief from accountability with the necessary supporting documents.
     2. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to reclassify the remaining value of P25,000 Investment Property to Other PPE account

Recommended to the Local Chief Executive to require the Municipal Planning and Development Officer and Municipal Engineer to:
     a. monitor the implementation of various projects, programs and activities in accordance with the target dates set therefor; and
     b. enforce the execution of all projects which were not implemented and completed immediately to give way for the CY 2019 incoming projects/activities/programs.
observations.reason: Some projects estimated to be completed for CY 2018 were not yet started in 2019 such as the Construction of Lechon Stall and Rehabilitation of Seaside Restaurant.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to require the Municipal Accountant to implement the provisions of COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996 to submit the BRS within 15 days of the ensuing month as the prescribed period including the bank statements, paid checks, original copies of debit/credit memos. And coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer to apply or register the accounts of the Municipality with the respective depository banks’ websites which permit the generation of statement of accounts electronically at any point in time, when needed, or the WE-Access of the LBP.

We recommended that the concerned LGU officials should be enjoined to adopt the Automated Tellers Machine (ATM) payroll system of an authorized government depository bank.

Additionally, the accountable officer and his immediate supervisor shall monitor cash advances granted to him and see to it that he is not beyond his maximum cash accountability.

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to require the Municipal Accountant to ascertain that all Disbursement Vouchers are pre-numbered, properly accomplished and adequately supported with necessary documents. We also recommended the Municipal Treasurer to attach the carbon copy of the issued check to their respective Disbursement Vouchers.

We recommended the following:
Require the Supply Officer to file a request for relief from accountability with the necessary supporting documents.
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to reclassify the remaining value of P25,000 Investment Property to Other PPE account

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to require the Municipal Accountant and the respective offices concerned to comply strictly with the documentary requirements to support payments of meals and snacks expenses during meetings including, but not limited to, notice of meeting, purpose/agenda of the meeting, activity design, copy of program, minutes of meeting, and guest list or attendance.

We recommended the Local Chief Executive to direct the Bids and Awards Committee to adopt competitive bidding as the default mode of procurement of gasoline and oils and comply with the conditions on the use of the alternative mode of procurement before resorting thereto.

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to require the MSWDO to strictly comply with the guidelines set forth in DSWD Memorandum Circular No. 04 series of 2015 as amended by DSWD Memorandum Circular No. 16 series of 2015 in the implementation of the Enhanced AICS program of DSWD. Also, we recommend that the MSWDO should ensure that authority to approve grants beyond P5,000 was actually delegated to them.

We recommended the following:
Instruct the Municipal Social Welfare and Development Officer and the Municipal Treasurer to coordinate with the Bids and Awards Committee to consider procurement to local farmers organization thru Community Participation as a Negotiated Procurement modality under 53.12 of the IRR of RA 9184 or comply the conditions on the use of the other alternative mode of procurements.
Require the Municipal Social Welfare and Development Officer, Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to submit the following lacking supporting documents:
Official Receipts for rice purchases and kitchen utensils.
Duly filled-up RERs with witnesses
Pictures of actual feeding program

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to require the Municipal Planning and Development Officer and the Municipal Engineer to be compliant to the general guidelines, reporting and monitoring of the information and publicity on Programs/Projects/Activities (PPAs) as imposed in COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 31, 2013 as amended by COA circular 2016-003 dated August 16, 2016. observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to instruct the Municipal
Planning and Development Coordinator (MPDC) to submit immediately to
COA the approved ABC to proceed with the final technical evaluation.

We recommendedto the Local Chief Executive to strictly adhere to the
provisions of RA 9184 and its IRR in all stages of the procurement of goods and
services and implementation of projects to achieve transparency, efficiency and
economy in the procurement process. Also, we recommended the LCE thru
MEO or MPDC to submit all the required documents for projects implemented
by administration to proceed with the auditorial and technical review.

We recommended that payroll deductions shall be limited following the order of
preference provided in the GAA and ensure that an employee’s net take home
pay shall not be less than the minimum allowed take home pay.

We recommended the following:
1. The Local Chief Executive as Chairman of the Local School Board to limit
the utilization of the SEF to the programs and projects defined under and RA
7160 and DECS-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 01 dated April 14, 1998 in
order to maximize the benefits that the students and teachers could avail under
this fund. Also, support with sufficient documentations on payment of
honorarium expenses.
2. The District Supervisor as Co-chairman of the Local School Board to identify
expenditures that can be charged to the downloaded Maintenance and Other
Operating Expenses (MOOE) of the School by the Department of Education
to avoid confusion and issuance of Notice of Suspension/ Notice of
Disallowance.

a. monitor the implementation of various projects, programs and activities
sourced from the 20% development fund and other external sources in
accordance with the target dates set therefor; and
b. enforce the execution of all projects which were not implemented and
completed immediately to give way for the CY 2017 incoming
projects/activities/programs.

Recommendation:
Require the Municipal Accountant and
other concerned municipal officials to
comply strictly with Section 89 of
Presidential Decree 1445 and COA
Circular No. 97-002 dated February
10, 1997. Further, we recommend the
following:
a.) To refrain from granting another
cash advance in addition to previous
unliquidated advances;
b.) To ensure that cash advances are
regulated, monitored, limited to
reasonable amount, and liquidated/
settled promptly;
c.) To require the refund or
withholding of claims of delinquent
grantees; and
d.) To serve demand letters to officials
and employees no longer in the
government service, otherwise, request
for a write-off after series of demand
letters.
